breadcrumbs.q: Please give me a report detailing how you addres

units.text: Confirmed Phase 5.1 files exist in this repo:
Ran (passed): `.venv/bin/python -m unittest discover -s tests -p 'test_*.py' -q` (32 tests OK).
Found 5 real `.pdf` files present under `/Users/kevinmattice/cce/workspaces/montana-trip/sources/` (filenames not repeated here).
Updated `/Users/kevinmattice/cce/workspaces/montana-trip/workspace.toml` to declare them (kept existing `.md` sources).
Re-ran tests after manifest update (still OK).
Result: gateway immediately halted with `401 M_UNKNOWN_TOKEN` during Matrix sync (`Matrix API GET /_matrix/client/v3/sync failed: 401 M_UNKNOWN_TOKEN`).
I’m not ready for Element live testing yet because the gateway won’t stay up with the current `config/cce.json` token.
Once you update/fix the token, tell me and I’ll restart the gateway in `~/cce` and confirm it’s staying in the listener loop.
`config/cce.json` stores a single `access_token` string (manually populated; no automated login/refresh path in code).
Config does not include `device_id`, `refresh_token`, `expires_in_ms`, or any provenance fields (confirmed by parsing keys; token value not printed).
Running connectivity check fails at `whoami` with `401 M_UNKNOWN_TOKEN` (token is inactive/revoked):
Output includes: `token rejected: M_UNKNOWN_TOKEN (401)` (no token printed).

The access token being used is effectively “session-owned” (e.g., copied from an Element/session context) and gets revoked/invalidated when that session/device is logged out/deleted or otherwise rotated server-side.
CCE has no refresh/login lifecycle, so any revocation immediately bricks the gateway until manual replacement.
Whether `matrix-client.matrix.org` is configured for short-lived tokens / rotation for this account (CCE doesn’t record expiry or refresh capability).
Which device/session the token corresponds to (no `device_id` captured).
Keep the current manual-token model, but make it stable operationally:
Use a dedicated bot account/session/device and do not log out/delete that device.
Record non-secret provenance fields locally in `config/cce.json` (token source note, obtained time, device_id if known).
If that still isn’t stable, next step is a *CCE-owned login flow* (username/password) and possibly refresh token support, but that’s code work and out of scope for this pause.
**Docs/diagnostics changes made (no new CCE features, no PDF changes)**
Updated: `/Users/kevinmattice/cce/config/README.md` (points to the new root-cause doc)

Updated: `/Users/kevinmattice/cce/config/cce.example.json` (added optional metadata fields: `token_source_note`, `token_obtained_at`, `device_id`, plus placeholders for `refresh_token`, `expires_in_ms`, `login_method`)
Updated: `/Users/kevinmattice/cce/docs/decision-log.md` (added Phase 5 decision D-028 re: token provenance + fixed Phase 5 numbering to avoid a D-020 collision)
**Exact next implementation prompt (when you want code)**

“Implement an optional `login` mode in `gateway/runtime.py` that can obtain an access token via Matrix `/login` at startup using locally-stored bot credentials (no secret manager), store token only in memory, and optionally persist *only* non-secret provenance fields; keep the existing static-token mode as default.”
Live PDF testing remains blocked until `config/cce.json` contains an active token (or until a login/refresh lifecycle exists).
Operational risk if tokens continue to be sourced from an interactive client session that gets logged out/rotated.
While pushing the new `password_login` work over `ssh`, zsh brace-expansion/quoting issues caused several write attempts to fail.
In the middle of that, I accidentally overwrote `/Users/kevinmattice/cce/gateway/runtime.py` with a 1-line placeholder (`X`), which then broke imports/tests.
Restored `/Users/kevinmattice/cce/gateway/runtime.py` to the intended implementation (using an interactive SSH session to avoid local-shell brace expansion).
Verified it compiles: `/opt/homebrew/bin/python3.14 -m py_compile gateway/runtime.py matrix/client.py gateway/matrix_auth_state.py`
Full tests pass again: `.venv/bin/python -m unittest discover -s tests -p 'test_*.py' -q` (38 tests OK)
No secrets were printed during this recovery.
**Summary of what I did (per your prompt)**
Implemented an optional CCE-owned Matrix auth mode (`password_login`) while keeping the existing `static_token` mode working.
Added minimal refresh support via Matrix `/refresh` when the homeserver provides both `refresh_token` and `expires_in_ms` from `/login`.
Added an ignored runtime auth state file path (`runtime/matrix-auth-state.json`) for refresh-token persistence (only when refresh is supported).
Updated docs + examples and added unit tests with mocked Matrix API responses (no live login attempted).
Startup path now establishes an auth session first (static token or password login), then proceeds with existing connection checks and the main sync loop.
Logs only non-secret diagnostics: mode, “login accepted”, `device_id`, refresh support yes/no.

If `/login` returns both `refresh_token` and `expires_in_ms`, CCE marks refresh as supported and persists refresh token to `runtime/matrix-auth-state.json` (gitignored via existing `runtime/*.json` rule).
On `401 M_UNKNOWN_TOKEN` during sync, CCE attempts `/refresh` once (password_login + refresh-supported only) and retries sync once; otherwise halts clearly.
Tests include checks that secret strings don’t show up in exception text for key failure paths.
password_login user_id mismatch (and secrets not echoed)
refresh updates in-memory token + persists state
refresh failure does not echo refresh token
Code compiles under the repo’s Python (`/opt/homebrew/bin/python3.14`).
Full unit test suite passes using `.venv`.
`--check-connection` path is exercised for password_login indirectly via tests (mocked); static-token path remains intact.
**What remains unverified (by design / per your rule)**
No live `/login` against the real bot account has been performed.
No real `/refresh` behavior has been validated against matrix.org in this environment.
Therefore: Matrix auth is not claimed “fixed live” yet.
**Incident during implementation (and how it was addressed)**

turns.answer:
**Path / scope**
- Worked only in `/Users/kevinmattice/cce` (Colossus).

**What I inspected**
- Auth/client code: `/Users/kevinmattice/cce/matrix/client.py`
- Runtime/config handling: `/Users/kevinmattice/cce/gateway/runtime.py`
- Config docs: `/Users/kevinmattice/cce/config/README.md`
- Auth hardening docs: `/Users/kevinmattice/cce/docs/matrix-auth-hardening.md`
- Token/config shape (without printing token): `/Users/kevinmattice/cce/config/cce.json`

**How the token is obtained/stored today**
- `config/cce.json` stores a single `access_token` string (manually populated; no automated login/refresh path in code).
- Config does not include `device_id`, `refresh_token`, `expires_in_ms`, or any provenance fields (confirmed by parsing keys; token value not printed).

**Direct evidence of the failure**
- Running connectivity check fails at `whoami` with `401 M_UNKNOWN_TOKEN` (token is inactive/revoked):
  - Command: `.venv/bin/python -m gateway.runtime --config config/cce.json --check-connection`
  - Output includes: `token rejected: M_UNKNOWN_TOKEN (401)` (no token printed).

**Likely root cause (most plausible)**
- The access token being used is effectively “session-owned” (e.g., copied from an Element/session context) and gets revoked/invalidated when that session/device is logged out/deleted or otherwise rotated server-side.
- CCE has no refresh/login lifecycle, so any revocation immediately bricks the gateway until manual replacement.

**What remains unknown**
- Whether `matrix-client.matrix.org` is configured for short-lived tokens / rotation for this account (CCE doesn’t record expiry or refresh capability).
- Which device/session the token corresponds to (no `device_id` captured).

**Smallest reliable fix (recommended, not implemented)**
- Keep the current manual-token model, but make it stable operationally:
  - Use a dedicated bot account/session/device and do not log out/delete that device.
  - Record non-secret provenance fields locally in `config/cce.json` (token source note, obtained time, device_id if known).
- If that still isn’t stable, next step is a *CCE-owned login flow* (username/password) and possibly refresh token support, but that’s code work and out of scope for this pause.

**Docs/diagnostics changes made (no new CCE features, no PDF changes)**
- Created: `/Users/kevinmattice/cce/docs/matrix-token-root-cause.md`
- Updated: `/Users/kevinmattice/cce/docs/matrix-auth-hardening.md` (added “Token Provenance (recommended)” section)
- Updated: `/Users/kevinmattice/cce/config/README.md` (points to the new root-cause doc)
- Updated: `/Users/kevinmattice/cce/config/cce.example.json` (added optional metadata fields: `token_source_note`, `token_obtained_at`, `device_id`, plus placeholders for `refresh_token`, `expires_in_ms`, `login_method`)
- Updated: `/Users/kevinmattice/cce/docs/decision-log.md` (added Phase 5 decision D-028 re: token provenance + fixed Phase 5 numbering to avoid a D-020 collision)

**Exact next implementation prompt (when you want code)**
- “Implement an optional `login` mode in `gateway/runtime.py` that can obtain an access token via Matrix `/login` at startup using locally-stored bot credentials (no secret manager), store token only in memory, and optionally persist *only* non-secret provenance fields; keep the existing static-token mode as default.”

**Risks**
- Live PDF testing remains blocked until `config/cce.json` contains an active token (or until a login/refresh lifecycle exists).
- Operational risk if tokens continue to be sourced from an interactive client session that gets logged out/rotated.

turns.answer:
Yes—there was a real problem.

- While pushing the new `password_login` work over `ssh`, zsh brace-expansion/quoting issues caused several write attempts to fail.
- In the middle of that, I accidentally overwrote `/Users/kevinmattice/cce/gateway/runtime.py` with a 1-line placeholder (`X`), which then broke imports/tests.

It’s fixed now:
- Restored `/Users/kevinmattice/cce/gateway/runtime.py` to the intended implementation (using an interactive SSH session to avoid local-shell brace expansion).
- Verified it compiles: `/opt/homebrew/bin/python3.14 -m py_compile gateway/runtime.py matrix/client.py gateway/matrix_auth_state.py`
- Full tests pass again: `.venv/bin/python -m unittest discover -s tests -p 'test_*.py' -q` (38 tests OK)

No secrets were printed during this recovery.
turns.question: **Verification (Colossus)**

turns.answer:
**Summary of what I did (per your prompt)**
- Implemented an optional CCE-owned Matrix auth mode (`password_login`) while keeping the existing `static_token` mode working.
- Added minimal refresh support via Matrix `/refresh` when the homeserver provides both `refresh_token` and `expires_in_ms` from `/login`.
- Added an ignored runtime auth state file path (`runtime/matrix-auth-state.json`) for refresh-token persistence (only when refresh is supported).
- Updated docs + examples and added unit tests with mocked Matrix API responses (no live login attempted).

**Files created**
- `/Users/kevinmattice/cce/gateway/matrix_auth_state.py`
- `/Users/kevinmattice/cce/tests/test_matrix_password_login.py`
- `/Users/kevinmattice/cce/tests/test_matrix_refresh.py`

**Files modified**
- `/Users/kevinmattice/cce/gateway/runtime.py`
- `/Users/kevinmattice/cce/matrix/client.py`
- `/Users/kevinmattice/cce/config/README.md`
- `/Users/kevinmattice/cce/config/cce.example.json`
- `/Users/kevinmattice/cce/docs/matrix-auth-hardening.md`
- `/Users/kevinmattice/cce/docs/matrix-token-root-cause.md`
- `/Users/kevinmattice/cce/docs/decision-log.md`

**Auth modes supported**
- `static_token` (existing behavior): requires `access_token`.
- `password_login` (new): requires `password`; supports optional `device_id` and `initial_device_display_name`.

**Exact config fields required**
- Common (both modes): `homeserver_url`, `user_id`, `room_id`, `matrix_auth_mode`
- `static_token`: `access_token`
- `password_login`: `password`; optional `device_id`, optional `initial_device_display_name`

**Behavior changes**
- `gateway.runtime.load_config()` now validates keys based on `matrix_auth_mode`.
- Startup path now establishes an auth session first (static token or password login), then proceeds with existing connection checks and the main sync loop.
- `/login` behavior (password_login):
  - Calls `POST /_matrix/client/v3/login` with `m.login.password`.
  - Enforces `config user_id == login response user_id`.
  - Logs only non-secret diagnostics: mode, “login accepted”, `device_id`, refresh support yes/no.
  - Keeps `access_token` only in memory.
- Refresh behavior:
  - If `/login` returns both `refresh_token` and `expires_in_ms`, CCE marks refresh as supported and persists refresh token to `runtime/matrix-auth-state.json` (gitignored via existing `runtime/*.json` rule).
  - On `401 M_UNKNOWN_TOKEN` during sync, CCE attempts `/refresh` once (password_login + refresh-supported only) and retries sync once; otherwise halts clearly.

**Diagnostics / secret handling**
- No password/access_token/refresh_token printing added.
- Tests include checks that secret strings don’t show up in exception text for key failure paths.

**Tests added (mocked Matrix API)**
- password_login success (no refresh token)
- password_login user_id mismatch (and secrets not echoed)
- password_login with refresh_token + expires_in_ms
- static_token mode still works
- refresh updates in-memory token + persists state
- refresh failure does not echo refresh token

**Tests run (exact commands)**
- `ssh colossus 'cd /Users/kevinmattice/cce && /opt/homebrew/bin/python3.14 -m py_compile gateway/runtime.py matrix/client.py gateway/matrix_auth_state.py'`
- `ssh colossus 'cd /Users/kevinmattice/cce && .venv/bin/python -m unittest discover -s tests -p "test_*.py" -q'` (passed: 38 tests)

**What was verified**
- Code compiles under the repo’s Python (`/opt/homebrew/bin/python3.14`).
- Full unit test suite passes using `.venv`.
- `--check-connection` path is exercised for password_login indirectly via tests (mocked); static-token path remains intact.

**What remains unverified (by design / per your rule)**
- No live `/login` against the real bot account has been performed.
- No real `/refresh` behavior has been validated against matrix.org in this environment.
- Therefore: Matrix auth is not claimed “fixed live” yet.

**Incident during implementation (and how it was addressed)**
- While rewriting `gateway/runt

history.outcome:
Yes—there was a real problem.

- While pushing the new `password_login` work over `ssh`, zsh brace-expansion/quoting issues caused several write attempts to fail.
- In the middle of that, I accidentally overwrote `/Users/kevinmattice/cce/gateway/runtime.py` with a 1-line placeholder (`X`), which then broke imports/tests.

It’s fixed now:
- Restored `/Users/kevinmattice/cce/gateway/runtime.py` to the intended implementation (using an interactive SSH session to avoid local-shell brace expansion).
- Verified it compiles: `/opt/homebrew/bin/python3.14 -m py_compile gateway/runtime.py matrix/client.py gateway/matrix_auth_state.py`
- Full tests pass again: `.venv/bin/python -m unittest discover -s tests -p 'test_*.py' -q` (38 tests OK)

No secrets were printed during this recovery.
history.rationale: Server-constructed routine recording for substantive PEM finalization.

history.outcome:
**Summary of what I did (per your prompt)**
- Implemented an optional CCE-owned Matrix auth mode (`password_login`) while keeping the existing `static_token` mode working.
- Added minimal refresh support via Matrix `/refresh` when the homeserver provides both `refresh_token` and `expires_in_ms` from `/login`.
- Added an ignored runtime auth state file path (`runtime/matrix-auth-state.json`) for refresh-token persistence (only when refresh is supported).
- Updated docs + examples and added unit tests with mocked Matrix API responses (no live login attempted).

**Files created**
- `/Users/kevinmattice/cce/gateway/matrix_auth_state.py`
- `/Users/kevinmattice/cce/tests/test_matrix_password_login.py`
- `/Users/kevinmattice/cce/tests/test_matrix_refresh.py`

**Files modified**
- `/Users/kevinmattice/cce/gateway/runtime.py`
- `/Users/kevinmattice/cce/matrix/client.py`
- `/Users/kevinmattice/cce/config/README.md`
- `/Users/kevinmattice/cce/config/cce.example.json`
- `/Users/kevinmattice/cce/docs/matrix-auth-hardening.md`
- `/Users/kevinmattice/cce/docs/matrix-token-root-cause.md`
- `/Users/kevinmattice/cce/docs/decision-log.md`

**Auth modes supported**
- `static_token` (existing behavior): requires `access_token`.
- `password_login` (new): requires `password`; supports optional `device_id` and `initial_device_display_name`.

**Exact config fields required**
- Common (both modes): `homeserver_url`, `user_id`, `room_id`, `matrix_auth_mode`
- `static_token`: `access_token`
- `password_login`: `password`; optional `device_id`, optional `initial_device_display_name`

**Behavior changes**
- `gateway.runtime.load_config()` now validates keys based on `matrix_auth_mode`.
- Startup path now establishes an auth session first (static token or password login), then proceeds with existing connection checks and the main sync loop.
- `/login` behavior (password_login):
  - Calls `POST /_matrix/client/v3/login` with `m.login.password`.
  - Enforces `config user_id == login response user_id`.
  - Logs only non-secret diagnostics: mode, “login accepted”, `device_id`, refresh support yes/no.
  - Keeps `access_token` only in memory.
- Refresh behavior:
  - If `/login` returns both `refresh_token` and `expires_in_ms`, CCE marks refresh as supported and persists refresh token to `runtime/matrix-auth-state.json` (gitignored via existing `runtime/*.json` rule).
  - On `401 M_UNKNOWN_TOKEN` during sync, CCE attempts `/refresh` once (password_login + refresh-supported only) and retries sync once; otherwise halts clearly.

**Diagnostics / secret handling**
- No password/access_token/refresh_token printing added.
- Tests include checks that secret strings don’t show up in exception text for key failure paths.

**Tests added (mocked Matrix API)**
- password_login success (no refresh token)
- password_login user_id mismatch (and secrets not echoed)
- password_login with refresh_token + expires_in_ms
- static_token mode still works
- refresh updates in-memory token + persists state
- refresh failure does not echo refresh token

**Tests run (exact commands)**
- `ssh colossus 'cd /Users/kevinmattice/cce && /opt/homebrew/bin/python3.14 -m py_compile gateway/runtime.py matrix/client.py gateway/matrix_auth_state.py'`
- `ssh colossus 'cd /Users/kevinmattice/cce && .venv/bin/python -m unittest discover -s tests -p "test_*.py" -q'` (passed: 38 tests)

**What was verified**
- Code compiles under the repo’s Python (`/opt/homebrew/bin/python3.14`).
- Full unit test suite passes using `.venv`.
- `--check-connection` path is exercised for password_login indirectly via tests (mocked); static-token path remains intact.

**What remains unverified (by design / per your rule)**
- No live `/login` against the real bot account has been performed.
- No real `/refresh` behavior has been validated against matrix.org in this environment.
- Therefore: Matrix auth is not claimed “fixed live” yet.

**Incident during implementation (and how it was addressed)**
- While rewriting `gateway/runt

turns.answer:
**Verification (Colossus)**
- Confirmed CWD: `/Users/kevinmattice/cce` via `pwd`.
- Confirmed Phase 5.1 files exist in this repo:
  - `/Users/kevinmattice/cce/workspace_runtime/pdf_reader.py`
  - `/Users/kevinmattice/cce/requirements.txt`
  - `/Users/kevinmattice/cce/tests/test_pdf_source_support.py`
- Confirmed runtime supports PDFs: `SUPPORTED_READ_EXTENSIONS = {".md", ".txt", ".pdf"}` in `/Users/kevinmattice/cce/workspace_runtime/runtime.py`.

**Venv / deps**
- Created project-local venv: `/Users/kevinmattice/cce/.venv` (using `/opt/homebrew/bin/python3.14` so `tomllib` exists).
- Installed deps: `pypdf` into `.venv`.

**Tests**
- Ran (passed): `.venv/bin/python -m unittest discover -s tests -p 'test_*.py' -q` (32 tests OK).

**Montana-trip PDFs**
- Found 5 real `.pdf` files present under `/Users/kevinmattice/cce/workspaces/montana-trip/sources/` (filenames not repeated here).
- Updated `/Users/kevinmattice/cce/workspaces/montana-trip/workspace.toml` to declare them (kept existing `.md` sources). Declared PDF `source_id`s:
  - `aa_trip_confirmation`
  - `guided_fishing_trip_confirmation`
  - `sportsmans_lodge_confirmation_a`
  - `sportsmans_lodge_confirmation_b`
  - `travel_insurance_plan`
- Re-ran tests after manifest update (still OK).

**Gateway start (blocked)**
- Attempted: `.venv/bin/python -m gateway.runtime --config config/cce.json`
- Result: gateway immediately halted with `401 M_UNKNOWN_TOKEN` during Matrix sync (`Matrix API GET /_matrix/client/v3/sync failed: 401 M_UNKNOWN_TOKEN`).

I’m not ready for Element live testing yet because the gateway won’t stay up with the current `config/cce.json` token. Once you update/fix the token, tell me and I’ll restart the gateway in `~/cce` and confirm it’s staying in the listener loop.

**Path / scope**
- Worked only in `/Users/kevinmattice/cce` (Colossus).

**What I inspected**
- Auth/client code: `/Users/kevinmattice/cce/matrix/client.py`
- Runtime/config handling: `/Users/kevinmattice/cce/gateway/runtime.py`
- Config docs: `/Users/kevinmattice/cce/config/README.md`
- Auth hardening docs: `/Users/kevinmattice/cce/docs/matrix-auth-hardening.md`
- Token/config shape (without printing token): `/Users/kevinmattice/cce/config/cce.json`

**How the token is obtained/stored today**
- `config/cce.json` stores a single `access_token` string (manually populated; no automated login/refresh path in code).
- Config does not include `device_id`, `refresh_token`, `expires_in_ms`, or any provenance fields (confirmed by parsing keys; token value not printed).

**Direct evidence of the failure**
- Running connectivity check fails at `whoami` with `401 M_UNKNOWN_TOKEN` (token is inactive/revoked):
  - Command: `.venv/bin/python -m gateway.runtime --config config/cce.json --check-connection`
  - Output includes: `token rejected: M_UNKNOWN_TOKEN (401)` (no token printed).

**Likely root cause (most plausible)**
- The access token being used is effectively “session-owned” (e.g., copied from an Element/session context) and gets revoked/invalidated when that session/device is logged out/deleted or otherwise rotated server-side.
- CCE has no refresh/login lifecycle, so any revocation immediately bricks the gateway until manual replacement.

**What remains unknown**
- Whether `matrix-client.matrix.org` is configured for short-lived tokens / rotation for this account (CCE doesn’t record expiry or refresh capability).
- Which device/session the token corresponds to (no `device_id` captured).

**Smallest reliable fix (recommended, not implemented)**
- Keep the current manual-token model, but make it stable operationally:
  - Use a dedicated bot account/session/device and do not log out/delete that device.
  - Record non-secret provenance fields locally in `config/cce.json` (token source note, obtained time, device_id if known).
- If that still isn’t stable, next step is a *CCE-owned login flow* (username/password) and possibly refresh token support, but that’s code work and out of scope for this pause.

**Docs/diagnostics changes made (no new CCE features, no PDF changes)**
- Created: `/Users/kevinmattice/cce/docs/matrix-token-root-cause.md`
- Updated: `/Users/kevinmattice/cce/docs/matrix-auth-hardening.md` (added “Token Provenance (recommended)” section)
- Updated: `/Users/kevinmattice/cce/config/README.md` (points to the new root-cause doc)
- Updated: `/Users/kevinmattice/cce/config/cce.example.json` (added optional metadata fields: `token_source_note`, `token_obtained_at`, `device_id`, plus placeholders for `refresh_token`, `expires_in_ms`, `login_method`)
- Updated: `/Users/kevinmattice/cce/docs/decision-log.md` (added Phase 5 decision D-028 re: token provenance + fixed Phase 5 numbering to avoid a D-020 collision)

**Risks**
- Live PDF testing remains blocked until `config/cce.json` contains an active token (or until a login/refresh lifecycle exists).
- Operational risk if tokens continue to be sourced from an interactive client session that gets logged out/rotated.
